vibe: City pop shimmer, Detroit deep house, Persian soul, Brazilian grooves, and experimental edges

tracks.name: Fly-By-Day
tracks.album: Crate Diggers: Japanese City Pop 1978-1987, Vol. 1
tracks.reason: City pop deep cut — adjacent to Taeko Onuki, Tatsuro Yamashita, and Masayoshi Takanaka in your rotation

tracks.name: Future Club
tracks.reason: Dark synthwave that sits right next to Gesaffelstein's lane — neon-drenched and menacing

tracks.name: Ey Sareban
tracks.reason: Persian singer-songwriter who reinvents traditional music — connects to your Googoosh and Homayoun Shajarian plays

tracks.name: Morning
tracks.reason: Brazilian jazz-funk trio — same world as Marcos Valle and Tim Maia but more synth-driven and groovy

tracks.name: Breathing Underwater
tracks.reason: Neo-soul/future jazz that bridges your Tom Misch and Yussef Dayes listening — Melbourne's finest

tracks.name: No One's Watching Me (feat. Olivia Dean)
tracks.reason: UK jazz collective in the Yussef Dayes orbit — infectious energy with Afrobeat undercurrents

tracks.name: Paradise Architects
tracks.reason: Detroit deep house royalty — same lineage as Moodymann and Larry Heard in your library

tracks.name: Automatic Love
tracks.reason: New wave R&B that channels your New Order and Sade energy through a lo-fi bedroom lens

tracks.name: Forerunner Foray
tracks.reason: Experimental hip-hop from Ishmael Butler — the warped, spacey counterpart to your Playboi Carti deep-dives

tracks.name: Talk to Me You'll Understand
tracks.reason: Lo-fi house that lives between your Four Tet and Floating Points worlds — warm, textured, hypnotic
